title: PowerShell Qualys API HTTP Client Patterns - Auth, Request Construction, And Response Parsing
applies_to: PowerShell scripts calling Qualys VMDR, WAS, or Asset Inventory APIs
Building reusable Qualys HTTP client wrapper functions in PowerShell
Parsing Qualys XML and JSON API responses in PowerShell

confirmed_facts.fact: Qualys API uses HTTP Basic Auth. Construct the Authorization header in PowerShell: `$encoded = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("${username}:${password}")); $headers = @{ Authorization = "Basic $encoded"; 'X-Requested-With' = 'PowerShell' }`. The X-Requested-With header is mandatory for all Qualys V2 API endpoints and must be a non-empty string.

Platform base URL varies by subscription pod. Identify your platform URL using `https://www.qualys.com/platform-identification` before coding. Common URLs: qualysapi.qualys.com (US1), qualysapi.qg2.apps.qualys.com (US2), qualysapi.qg3.apps.qualys.com (US3), qualysapi.eu.apps.qualys.com (EU1). Hardcode the resolved URL; do not auto-detect at runtime in production.

VMDR Host Detection API call in PowerShell: `$response = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://$qualysBase/api/2.0/fo/asset/host/vm/detection/?action=list&truncation_limit=1000" -Headers $headers -Method GET`. Returns XML. PowerShell's Invoke-RestMethod auto-parses XML to an XmlDocument object when Content-Type is application/xml.

Navigate Qualys XML response in PowerShell: `$xml.HOST_LIST_VM_DETECTION_OUTPUT.RESPONSE.HOST_LIST.HOST` gives the array of host elements. Each HOST contains `IP`, `DNS`, `DETECTION_LIST` with child `DETECTION` elements. Access nested values: `$host.DETECTION_LIST.DETECTION | ForEach-Object { $_.QID, $_.SEVERITY }.

Global IT Asset Inventory API uses JSON and POST method. PowerShell call: `$body = '{"ServiceRequest":{"preferences":{"startFromOffset":0,"limitResults":100}}}'; $response = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://$qualysBase/qps/rest/2.0/search/am/asset" -Headers $headers -Method POST -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"`. Returns PSCustomObject from JSON.

Global IT Asset Inventory pagination: check `$response.ServiceResponse.hasMoreRecords`. If `true`, increment `startFromOffset` by `limitResults` and repeat the call. Continue until `hasMoreRecords` is `false` or the returned `count` is less than `limitResults`.

VMDR detection pagination: use `id_min` filter incremented to the last returned host ID. Example: append `&id_min=$lastId` to the request URI on each subsequent call. Alternatively use `since_datetime` to fetch only changed detections since a timestamp.

WAS API (Web Application Scanning) call in PowerShell: `$body = '{"ServiceRequest":{"preferences":{"startFromOffset":0,"limitResults":100}}}'; Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://$qualysBase/qps/rest/3.0/search/was/webapp" -Headers $headers -Method POST -Body $body -ContentType "application/json"`. Response: `$response.ServiceResponse.data.WebApp`.

Rate limits: Qualys enforces per-module hourly call limits (approximately 300 requests/hour for VMDR per user). HTTP 429 responses indicate rate limit exceeded. Qualys does not reliably provide a Retry-After header; default to a 60-second sleep on 429 responses.

implementation_notes: Build a reusable `Invoke-QualysRequest` function that accepts URI, method, and optional body, handles header construction, detects response format (XML vs JSON by Content-Type), and includes retry logic for 429 and 503 responses.
Resolve and cache the platform base URL as a script-level variable `$script:QualysBase` set once at script startup, not re-resolved on every call.
Convert Qualys XML detection data to PSCustomObjects early in the pipeline using `Select-Xml` or XPath expressions, then work with objects rather than XML nodes throughout the rest of the script.
For bulk asset or detection exports, prefer `since_datetime` filters over full dumps to reduce API call volume and response size on incremental runs.
Validate that the X-Requested-With header value is a non-empty string. Some PowerShell versions may silently drop headers with null or empty values.
caveats: X-Requested-With header is mandatory for all V2 API calls. Omitting it returns 403 Forbidden with no meaningful error body.
Platform URL is subscription-specific. Using the wrong pod URL returns authentication errors that look identical to wrong credentials.
Some Qualys modules return XML even when output_format=JSON is specified in the query string. Always check Content-Type of the response before parsing.
The VMDR detection endpoint truncates results at the specified truncation_limit (max 1000 per call). Always implement pagination for production environments with large host counts.
Qualys session-based auth (via /api/2.0/fo/session/ login endpoint) is an alternative but stateful model. Prefer stateless Basic Auth for unattended scripts to avoid session management complexity.
